rationale: Keep: the cited paper is highly topically aligned with the citing paper (both study when emotional language helps vs backfires in marketing communication), and the citing paper’s own note explicitly positions itself as refining prior social-media emotionality work with a “backfire effect.” There is direct construct overlap on hedonic/product-type moderation (cited: hedonic vs utilitarian boundary condition; citing: effect reduced for hedonic products), suggesting likely substantive theoretical use rather than incidental mention.
